## Article Ideas

- **Gemini CLI Sunset Timeline:** Multiple sources confirm Gemini CLI deprecation
  on June 18, 2026, across AI Pro, AI Ultra, and free tiers. A comparison
  article on migration strategies would be valuable for the target audience.
- **Antigravity vs Competitive Positioning:** Nyndra AI's analysis and Matt Kuda's
  build walkthrough both frame Antigravity 2.0 as a direct Codex and Claude Code
  competitor. Could expand into a competitive analysis article.
- **Multi-Agent Teams in Practice:** The official Beginner's Guide highlights
  multi-agent teams as a core differentiator. Potential article on agent
  orchestration patterns.
- **Antigravity CLI vs Desktop App:** Multiple videos distinguish between the
  terminal CLI and standalone desktop application. A guide comparing use cases
  would serve the target audience well.

- **Free Tier Access to Antigravity 2.0:** Multiple new videos (Income stream
  surfers, Data Science in your pocket) cover free access to Antigravity 2.0
  with Gemini 3.5 Flash. A practical guide on free tier capabilities and
  limitations would help cost-conscious developers.
- **Antigravity IDE Downgrade & Version Management:** MyCodeWorks published a
  guide to switching back to the old IDE version after the I/O 2026 update,
  indicating community concern about breaking changes. Coverage of version
  management could help users navigate platform transitions.
- **Agent OS Template & Workflow Patterns:** Rachel noCode introduced a free
  Agent OS template with briefs, task queues, and safety rules. An article
  on operational patterns for managing multi-agent workflows would complement
  the beginner content already curated.
- **Gemini 3.5 Flash as Antigravity Backend:** Codex Community and Income
  stream surfers highlight Gemini 3.5 Flash with its 1M token context window
  as the backend for Antigravity. An article on how model capabilities affect
  agent performance would add technical depth to the coverage.
